content.body:
The wording for the `update href` algorithm https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#api-for-a-and-area-elements:update-href seems a bit odd (I'll abbreviate  HTMLAnchorElement and HTMLAreaElement as X and Y respectively): "To update href for X and Y, ..."
Instead, it could be
- "To update href for an X or Y element, ..." or
- "The update href algorithm for X and Y elements is ..." (like the `set the url` algorithm that immediately follows it).
content.formatted_body:
The wording for the update href algorithm https://html.spec.whatwg.org/#api-for-a-and-area-elements:update-href seems a bit odd (I'll abbreviate HTMLAnchorElement and HTMLAreaElement as X and Y respectively): "To update href for X and Y, ..."
Instead, it could be

  • "To update href for an X or Y element, ..." or
  • "The update href algorithm for X and Y elements is ..." (like the set the url algorithm that immediately follows it).

content.body: jmdyck: I like the one starting with "To ...". I might do "To update href given an X or Y element _element_:"
content.formatted_body: jmdyck: I like the one starting with "To ...". I might do "To update href given an X or Y element element:"

content.body: Regrets for today's meeting, I have a conflict
Oh btw, could I get "triage" role in the HTML repo so I can `agenda+` issues without having to ask every time to my collagues 🙏
